nativeRange.habitat: Shallow, slow-moving or still blackwater streams, peat swamps, and forest pools with soft, acidic water; substrate of fine sand and leaf litter with dense overhanging vegetation

decorPreferences: leaf litter (Indian almond leaves, oak leaves)
dense planting including Java fern, Anubias, and mosses
dim or shaded areas — subdued or low-wattage lighting

diet.notes: Accepts sinking wafers, micro-pellets, and bottom-feeder tablets. Supplement with frozen or live foods such as bloodworms, tubifex, daphnia, and brine shrimp. Scavenges uneaten food and detritus from the substrate. Best fed after lights out due to nocturnal activity. Avoid large-particle foods that cannot be reached under sand.

goodWith: freshwater shrimp (larger species — cherry shrimp may be at risk from adults)

summary: The Black Kuhli Loach (Pangio oblonga) is a uniformly dark, eel-like loach from Southeast Asian blackwater streams. Unlike the banded Kuhli Loach (P. kuhlii), it lacks body bands and is entirely dark brown to black. Hardy and peaceful; best kept in groups of 5+ with fine sand and low lighting.
careNotes: The Black Kuhli Loach is frequently confused with the standard Kuhli Loach (Pangio kuhlii), which displays distinctive yellow-and-brown banding. P. oblonga is uniformly dark brown to black with no banding — a key identification feature. Like all kuhli-type loaches it has a smooth, scaleless-like body with very small embedded scales, making it highly sensitive to copper-based medications and salt treatments; always use copper-free alternatives and half-doses for any medication. The species is almost exclusively nocturnal: it hides under substrate, rocks, or in dense planting during the day and becomes active after lights-off. A fine sand substrate of at least 5 cm depth is strongly preferred, allowing the fish to burrow and bury itself completely — coarse gravel can abrade the delicate underside. A group of 5 or more is strongly recommended; smaller numbers result in extreme shyness, infrequent feeding, and stress. Provide numerous hiding spots (driftwood, caves, leaf litter, PVC pipe) to encourage activity and reduce hiding. Water chemistry should be soft and slightly acidic to neutral, matching the blackwater peat-swamp biotopes of Borneo and the Malay Peninsula. A tight-fitting lid is essential — kuhli-type loaches are escape-prone and can squeeze through surprisingly small gaps. Standard aquarium salt should be avoided given their scaleless-like sensitivity.
breedingNotes: Breeding in captivity is rarely reported and considered difficult. Successful spawns have occurred in large, heavily planted, soft-acidic tanks with a reduced water level and simulated rainy-season conditions (cool water change triggering spawning). Eggs are small, green, and scattered among floating plants. Fry are tiny and require infusoria or liquid fry food. Commercial production relies on wild collection; captive breeding is not routine in the hobby.

primary.name: SeriouslyFish — Pangio oblonga
primary.notes: Primary source. Provided adult size (~10 cm), temperature range (24–30°C), pH (5.5–7.0), hardness, native range (Malaysia, Indonesia, Singapore, Thailand), blackwater/peat-swamp biotope, nocturnal and burrowing behaviour, group requirements, diet, and breeding notes. Confirmed solid dark coloration distinguishing P. oblonga from banded P. kuhlii.

additional.name: FishBase — Pangio oblonga
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Cobitidae, Cypriniformes), adult size (to 8 cm SL, approximately 10 cm TL), native range (Borneo, Malay Peninsula), temperature (24–28°C), and diet classification (omnivore). Confirmed scientific name and family placement.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op — Kuhli Loach Care Guide
additional.notes: Trade care guide. Confirmed easy care level, nocturnal behaviour, sand substrate requirement, group of 5+ recommendation, copper sensitivity (scaleless-like body), and feeding approach. Notes banded vs. solid-dark forms and distinguishes P. kuhlii from P. oblonga in passing.

additional.name: Practical Fishkeeping — Kuhli Loach species profile
additional.notes: Supplemental source. Confirmed burrowing behaviour and sand substrate importance, nocturnal feeding schedule, group social requirements, breeding rarity in captivity, and water chemistry preferences. Also notes escape risk via tight-fitting lid advice.

additional.name: Seriously Fish — Pangio kuhlii (comparative reference)
additional.notes: Comparative reference used to confirm husbandry parity between P. kuhlii and P. oblonga and to document the banded vs. solid-dark distinction. Parameters and care requirements are effectively identical between the two species.

fish.breedingNotes: Rarely bred in captivity. Requires large, heavily planted soft-acidic tank, reduced water level, and simulated rainy-season cool-water trigger. Eggs scattered among floating plants; fry require infusoria. Commercial stock is predominantly wild-caught.
